title: Insulin Tolerance Test in Mouse
author: Nancy Smith
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wxjffkn

steps.title: Fasting begins first thing in the morning (around 9am)
steps.instruction: Fasting begins first thing in the morning (around 9am). Fast mice for 4-6 hours before ITT begins. Transfer mice to clean cage and wire top. Keep water bottles during the fasting period. If using High Fat diet (HFD), save food to give back at the end of the ITT. Mark tails with a sharpie for easier identification during ITT.

steps.title: Weigh mouse to obtain body weight for dose calculations
steps.instruction: Weigh mouse to obtain body weight for dose calculations. Calculate bolus dose of insulin ( 1-1.2 units/kg ). Weight of mouse (g) x 10 = ul insulin stock 2 solution ITT - template spreadsheet.xlsx

steps.title: Prepare syringes using 1cc syringe and 26G needle
steps.instruction: Prepare syringes using 1cc syringe and 26G needle. Load all syringes with calculated insulin dose and position in front of home cage.

steps.title: Prior to the delivery of the...
steps.instruction: Prior to the delivery of the insulin solution, a time “zero” blood glucose level must be measured. Restrain mouse and clean tail with 70% ethanol/gauze. Extract a small amount of blood from the tail vein onto One Touch Ultra blood glucose strips used with the One Touch Ultra 2 blood glucose monitoring system.

steps.title: Administer the correct dose of Insulin...
steps.instruction: Administer the correct dose of Insulin solution using an IP (intraperitoneal) injection method. Restrain the mouse. Then place the mouse is in dorsal recumbency with the head down allowing the viscera to move cranially. Using the bend of the knee and the ventral abdominal midline as landmarks, the needle is inserted half way between the midline and lateral side of the animal at the natural bend of the knee. Insert the needle at a 30 to 45 degree angle into either the left or right lower abdominal quadrant. Prior to injection, aspirate to make sure the needle has not penetrated a blood vessel, the intestines or the urinary bladder. When aspirating the syringe, you should see an air bubble in the hub of the needle, and not any form of fluid. Inject and return mouse to cage.

steps.title: Begin timing from the point of...
steps.instruction: Begin timing from the point of successfully delivering the insulin solution.

steps.title: Test and record the blood glucose...
steps.instruction: Test and record the blood glucose level (see step 5) at times 15, 30, 60, 90, and 120 minutes after the initial glucose delivery. Record each value on Insulin Tolerance Test record sheet (template found on step 2). Record condition of animal throughout and after procedure on the Insulin Tolerance Test recored sheet.

steps.title: Return food and environmental enrichments to cages
steps.instruction: Return food and environmental enrichments to cages.

steps.title: Stock 1
steps.instruction: Stock 1: 50ul of 100U/ml Humulin R into 4950ul of cold sterile 1X PBS. Invert solution 3-4 times to mix. Stock 2: 1ml of Stock 1 into 9ml of cold sterile 1X PBS. Invert solution 3-4 times to mix. Keep on ice until use
